The fix addresses the F-HALT-SMOKE-RUN-8 sole remaining skip — Case 12 `rate-limit halt fires with class 5 via adapter` — via a future 2-file Relay edit (1) to `src/verify/limits.js` aligning the gate-8 verifier with the canonical `{ maxPerWindow, windowMs }` object shape validated by `src/runtime/rate-limit-state.js`, and (2) to `tests/smoke/12-rate-limit-adapter-halt.test.js` reactivating the test as an AMENDMENT-7-style non-boot direct gate-verifier test. Root cause: `src/verify/limits.js:83` compares `current >= channelCap` where `channelCap` is the canonical object `{ maxPerWindow, windowMs }` (not a scalar); the number-vs-object comparison coerces to NaN and always returns false, so gate-8 never fires halt class 5. Proposed Option C: change `current >= channelCap.maxPerWindow`, emit `params: { channelCap: channelCap.maxPerWindow, currentCount: current }`, update stale line-44 comment to ASCII-only object-shape wording per CASE-09 SAFE IMPLEMENTATION ASCII-only precedent (no Unicode `§`), preserve `haltClassOverride: 5` and character-limit branch unchanged, reactivate Case 12 with direct `errors[0].haltClassOverride === 5` assertion (cleaner than AMENDMENT-7 keyword-mapping because limits gate's error envelope includes haltClassOverride field). SCAFFOLD-REPAIR scope (Relay-repo): exactly 6 files modified — 3 helpers (`tests/smoke/helpers/temp-tree.js`, `tests/smoke/helpers/synthetic-runtime-config.js`, `tests/smoke/helpers/synthetic-message.js`) + 3 smoke tests (`tests/smoke/06-gate-5-idempotency-collision-halt.test.js` as 1-line literal replacement, `tests/smoke/07-halt-publish-log-append.test.js` as whole-file non-boot rewrite, `tests/smoke/08-pending-move-after-halt.test.js` as whole-file non-boot rewrite); 98 insertions(+), 161 deletions(-); zero `src/` touch, zero `schemas/` touch, zero `package.json` / `package-lock.json` byte change, zero parent-repo touch during implementation, zero env / secrets touch, zero test execution this phase, zero deploy. Implementation details: temp-tree.js adds `await fs.writeFile(ceilingPauseSignal, 'ACTIVE');` after AMENDMENT-4's publish-log + dry-run-log writeFile calls (ceiling-pause-signal fixture, +1 line, AMENDMENT-3 sealPending helper preserved verbatim); synthetic-runtime-config.js adds `import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';` + `schemaPath: fileURLToPath(new URL('../../../schemas/hermes-message.schema.json', import.meta.url))` to runtime config and flips `allowedChannels` from `['#smoke-test-allowed']` (plain array) to `{ '#status': '123456789012345678' }` (object map matching `src/verify/channel-allowlist.js:54`/`:62` contract); synthetic-message.js rewrite makes `shortMessageId()` emit uppercase IDs `SMOKE-<8-hex-upper>` matching schema pattern `^[A-Z][A-Z0-9-]{4,63}$`, rewrites `buildSyntheticMessage` default to satisfy `schemas/hermes-message.schema.json` 9 required fields (`message_id`, `channel_id: '123456789012345678'`, `channel_name: '#status'`, `body`, `codex_pass_verdict_ref { verdict: 'PASS', review_id, reviewed_at }`, `operator_authorization { mode: 'per-message', approver: 'Victor', approved_at, approval_session_ref }`, `allowed_placeholder_map: {}`, `halt_on_condition_flags { ceiling_pause_must_be_active: true }`, `dry_run: false`) with `additionalProperties: false` honored (no `schema_version`, no `requested_at`, no `codex_pass`), and updates 3 variants to each apply exactly ONE targeted invalidity (`buildSchemaInvalidMessage` deletes `body` → gate-1 halt 29; `buildChannelDisallowedMessage` sets `channel_name: '#summaries'` schema-valid-but-not-in-allowlist → gate-2 halt 3; `buildStaleApprovalMessage` sets `operator_authorization.approved_at` 25h ago → gate-4 halt 2); Case 06 changes only the message_id override from `'msg-collision-x'` (lowercase) to `'MSG-COLLISION-X'` (uppercase, schema-valid) — preserves AMENDMENT-3 `await tempTree.sealPending();` call verbatim + boot import + env mutation block + pre-populated publish-log success record + halt-class-7 assertion; Case 07 + Case 08 whole-file rewrites bypass `boot()` and `sealPending()` per Path D (Case 07 directly invokes `createPublishLogWriter().ensureLogIntegrity()` + `.append(haltRecord)` and asserts canonical §14 6-field shape per `src/store/publish-log.js:194-197`; Case 08 directly invokes `createMessageStore().moveToProcessed(filename)` happy-path then sub-case forces rename failure via `fs.chmod(processed, 0o500)` and asserts thrown `StoreError(haltClass: 24, reason: /^rename-failed/)` per `src/store/source-of-truth.js:186-191`). ## ARC Governance Activation (ARC-GO-LIVE)

**Mode:** DOCS-ONLY. Committed and pushed at `de91d325b8160fb8183cc26172e50f3f35831796`; ARC-1 through ARC-7 are the active orchestrator governance control layer.

ARC-GO-LIVE is the orchestrator-governance activation checkpoint. It officially activates ARC-1 through ARC-7 as the active control layer for Agent Avila and confirms the governance framework binds all subsequent work. **ARC-GO-LIVE does not equal live trading. It does not authorize any production mutation, migration application, deploy, Railway command, live Kraken action, env / secret read or write, `MANUAL_LIVE_ARMED` change, package / lockfile edit, runtime edit, or `position.json` change.**

Active governance controls:

| ARC | Doc | Function |
|---|---|---|
| ARC-1 | `orchestrator/PROTECTED-FILES.md` | Per-path SAFE / RESTRICTED / HARD BLOCK matrix; Ruflo / future-automation rule |
| ARC-2 | `orchestrator/APPROVAL-GATES.md` | 16-gate action-class matrix; "what is NOT operator approval" non-equivalence list |
| ARC-3 | `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md` | Six phase modes (READ-ONLY AUDIT / DESIGN-ONLY / DOCS-ONLY / SAFE IMPLEMENTATION / HIGH-RISK IMPLEMENTATION / PRODUCTION ACTION) |
| ARC-4 | `orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION-SELECTOR.md` | Ten ordered selector rules; master-order discipline |
| ARC-5 | `orchestrator/ROLE-HIERARCHY.md` | Five named roles (Victor / ChatGPT / Gemini / Claude / Codex) + future-automation governance-only inheritance |
| ARC-6 | `orchestrator/AUTOMATION-PERMISSIONS.md` | GREEN / YELLOW / RED three-tier permission model; Claude / Codex / ChatGPT / Gemini / Ruflo / Relay / successors all governance-only |
| ARC-7 | `orchestrator/HANDOFF-RULES.md` + `orchestrator/handoffs/` | Packet rules and templates; packets cannot approve, cannot transport, cannot trade |

**Confirmed at activation:**

1. **ARC governance is not live trading and is not production mutation.** ARC-GO-LIVE is a docs-only governance step. The trading-runtime separation rule from `orchestrator/ROLE-HIERARCHY.md` "Critical separation rule" and `orchestrator/AUTOMATION-PERMISSIONS.md` "Critical separation rule" remains in force: the brains govern changes; they do not run the runtime.
2. **Victor / CEO remains the sole final authority.** All RED-tier actions (per `AUTOMATION-PERMISSIONS.md`), all 16 numbered approval gates (per `APPROVAL-GATES.md`), all phase-mode promotions (per `PHASE-MODES.md`), all master-order changes (per `NEXT-ACTION-SELECTOR.md`), all scoped lifts on RESTRICTED / HARD BLOCK files (per `PROTECTED-FILES.md`), and all commits of safety-policy docs require explicit, in-session, per-action approval from Victor.
3. **No AI role can self-approve.** Claude (Lead Engineer / Builder), Codex (Chief Risk & Safety Officer), Gemini (Director of Architecture / UX), ChatGPT (VP of Strategy & Orchestration), and any future automation layer (Ruflo, Relay, successors) are governance-only and cannot grant themselves authority. Codex PASS, clean `git status`, green tests, scheduled triggers, signed tokens, CI status, and any LLM self-approval DO NOT satisfy any operator-approval gate (per `APPROVAL-GATES.md` "What is NOT operator approval" and `AUTOMATION-PERMISSIONS.md` Tier 3 — RED rule).
4. **The 3-brains workflow does not enter the trading runtime hot path.** Live order decisions are made by `bot.js` + the operator + Kraken — not by Claude / Codex / ChatGPT / Gemini / Ruflo / Relay. Automation may freely operate within GREEN-tier read-only verification, draft proposed changes for higher tiers, and surface mode-promotion requests to Victor — but cannot apply, commit, deploy, or execute outside an explicit operator instruction.
5. **N-phase production work was confirmed blocked behind its existing gates at ARC-GO-LIVE activation, and has since advanced through the proper N-3 gate via Attempt 6 SUCCESS.** As of 2026-05-04, N-3 is closed and Migration 008 is APPLIED to production at HEAD `189eb1be6ef6304d914671bdaedec44d389cf877`, after the §11 five-condition gate was independently satisfied (Codex docs PASS, fresh Codex N-3 preflight PASS at HEAD `189eb1b…`, fresh Victor in-session production-action approval naming `189eb1b…`, all eleven §4 (i)–(xi) checks at execution time, target Railway service + production DB confirmation). ARC-GO-LIVE did not advance, bypass, or alter the N-3 gate; the gate was satisfied through the proper N-3 sequence. Migration 009+ would require a separate runbook + Codex review + fresh Victor approval.
6. **Prior production-action approvals remain CONSUMED and cannot be reused.** All six prior approvals (`9ae139d` consumed by Attempt 1 halt, `3138e7f` by Attempt 2 halt, `e7bce59…` by Attempt 3 halt, `e7bce59…` by Attempt 4 halt, `726b2e3…` by Attempt 5 halt, `189eb1be6ef6304d914671bdaedec44d389cf877` by Attempt 6 — succeeded) cannot be applied to any future migration attempt. Migration 009+ would require a separate runbook + Codex review + fresh Victor in-session production-action approval at that future HEAD, after a separate post-commit deploy-and-verify cycle.
7. **Future work obeys ARC-1 through ARC-7 without exception.** Every subsequent phase must (a) be labeled with exactly one mode per `PHASE-MODES.md` before any state-mutating tool call, (b) respect the per-path matrix in `PROTECTED-FILES.md` (SAFE / RESTRICTED / HARD BLOCK), (c) clear the relevant gates in the `APPROVAL-GATES.md` 16-gate matrix, (d) follow the strict ordering in `NEXT-ACTION-SELECTOR.md`'s ten selector rules, (e) respect the role hierarchy in `ROLE-HIERARCHY.md` (CEO is sole approver; brains recommend / review / block but do not approve), (f) operate within the GREEN / YELLOW / RED tiers in `AUTOMATION-PERMISSIONS.md`, and (g) honor the packet rules in `HANDOFF-RULES.md` (packets are not approval channels, not transports, not trading interfaces, and never override the canonical sources).

**ARC-GO-LIVE explicitly does not authorize:** deploying, running migrations, running Railway commands, modifying runtime files (`bot.js`, `dashboard.js`, `db.js`, `migrations/**`, `scripts/**`, `position.json`, `package.json`, lockfiles, `.env` files, deploy config), reading or writing env / secrets, changing `MANUAL_LIVE_ARMED`, touching live Kraken paths, executing the N-3 migration application, deploying the latest committed HEAD, or any other RED-tier action. The activation is committed and pushed at `de91d325b8160fb8183cc26172e50f3f35831796`; subsequent RED-tier actions still require their own per-action explicit Victor / CEO approval per `APPROVAL-GATES.md` and `AUTOMATION-PERMISSIONS.md`.

## N-3 Gate (closed)

**The N-3 gate for Migration 008 was satisfied on 2026-05-04 at HEAD `189eb1be6ef6304d914671bdaedec44d389cf877` via Attempt 6 SUCCESS.** Migration 008 is APPLIED to production; the Attempt 6 production-action approval was CONSUMED; no current N-3 preflight is pending; no further Migration 008 action is authorized. The canonical historical record of how Attempt 6 cleared each of the five gate conditions lives in runbook `orchestrator/handoffs/N-2-MIGRATION-008-PRODUCTION-PLAN.md` §11.

**Future migration applications (Migration 009 or later) are NOT authorized by this status and cannot reuse the Migration 008 approval path.** They require their own separate runbook, separate Codex docs-only review, separate fresh Codex N-3 preflight at the relevant future HEAD, and separate fresh Victor in-session production-action approval, plus the same-session §4 (i)–(xi) pre-flight checks at execution time per the N-2m / N-2x / N-2y / N-2z / N-2u / N-2t / N-2o / N-2n / N-2l / N-2k rules. The five-condition gate structure remains in force as the structural template for any such future track:

- Codex PASS on the runbook for that future migration.
- Fresh Codex N-3 preflight PASS on that runbook at the relevant committed HEAD.
- Fresh Victor in-session production-action approval naming the exact full HEAD at that time.
- All eleven §4 pre-flight checks (i) through (xi) PASS at execution time.
- Target Railway service and production database confirmation without exposing secrets.

The Migration 008 path of these conditions is consumed and cannot be substituted for any future migration's gate.

All six prior Victor production-action approvals are **CONSUMED** and cannot be reused (Attempt 6 succeeded; the consumption rule applies regardless of outcome):

- Approval naming `9ae139d` was consumed by N-3 attempt 1, which halted before SQL execution.
- Approval naming `3138e7f` was consumed by N-3 attempt 2, which halted before SQL execution.
- Approval naming `e7bce599df678222ab0a58f0a4f3cc9f562f509a` (Attempt 3) was consumed by N-3 attempt 3, which halted at the pre-SQL preflight tooling stage; the runner was not invoked.
- Approval naming `e7bce599df678222ab0a58f0a4f3cc9f562f509a` (Attempt 4) was consumed by N-3 attempt 4, which halted at the pre-SQL preflight tooling stage with the same root cause (recurrence + host-vs-container command confusion); the runner was not invoked.
- Approval naming `726b2e33988b4ea4f31f94a3361144d3ce8e8fac` (Attempt 5) was consumed by N-3 attempt 5, which halted at the pre-SQL preflight tooling stage on a NEW failure mode (ESM/CommonJS mismatch: Claude-supplied `/app/check_vii_abc.js` used CommonJS `require(...)` while `/app/package.json` declares `"type": "module"`); the runner was not invoked.
- Approval naming `189eb1be6ef6304d914671bdaedec44d389cf877` (Attempt 6) was consumed by N-3 attempt 6, which **SUCCEEDED**: runner exit 0; Migration 008 applied to production; `emergency_audit_log` table created with all 13 columns, 6 indexes, PK on `id`, UNIQUE on `event_id`, CHECK on `mode IN ('paper','live')`, two partial indexes (unresolved, kraken_order_id), and zero rows; `schema_migrations.version = 8` row inserted with `name = 'emergency_audit_log'` (per runner `parseFile` semantics — numeric prefix stripped) and a non-null SHA-256 `checksum`. All 10 structural §6 verifications PASS; the lone `SIX-A-VERSION-8-ROW:FAIL` label was a benign verifier-script expectation mismatch (verifier expected `name` to contain `"008"`; actual stored value is `'emergency_audit_log'`) corrected by N-2aa per Codex closeout review.

## Required Carry-Forward Gaps

**Check D — repo-side pin in place via Option A.** A repo-root `.nvmrc` containing the exact normalized value `24.10.0` is committed, satisfying runbook §4(x)(b) source priority 1 (highest). The pin matches the deployed runtime as captured at fact-finding time: in-container `node --version` from a Railway SSH session into the production `agent-avila-dashboard` container returned `v24.10.0`, normalized per the §4(x)(b) parsing-hygiene rule (strip leading `v`, strip trailing whitespace / newlines) to `24.10.0`. `package.json` `engines.node` remains the non-exact range `">=18.0.0"` and is unchanged in this phase; per the canonical-source rule the highest-priority present source (`.nvmrc`) is canonical, with HALT-on-disagreement against any other present source. **Check D verified PASS at deployed HEAD `49650f077509d83dcbf3e9771dc9ca30f351e55b` on 2026-05-04** via Web UI deploy-identity capture (GitHub-push-tracked, full 40-char SHA on Railway non-secret surface) plus Railway SSH in-container `node --version` = `v24.10.0` (normalized `24.10.0`) byte-for-byte equal to `.nvmrc`. **N-2r-preflight freshness rule remains in force** and is now codified in runbook §4(x)(b) by N-2u (Codex Q10 required edit). **Check D re-verification at HEAD `0a6974884849dd1eb6bdf4f88cb5d41085044612` (post-N-2u GitHub-tracked Railway redeploy; deployment ID `3355d5ee`, status Active) = PASS** via Railway SSH in-container `node --version` = `v24.10.0` (normalized `24.10.0`, byte-for-byte equal to `.nvmrc`). Container `root@ee8605893ce8:/app`; `pwd` = `/app`; `.nvmrc` present in `/app` (`-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 8 May 3 23:26 .nvmrc`). Per the §4(x)(b) freshness rule, this re-verification will be invalidated by the next intervening deploy; a fresh Check D re-verification at the post-N-2z deployed HEAD per `git rev-parse HEAD`, attested by the operator in the next Codex N-3 preflight packet (per the N-2z-codified §4(x) Evidence-record form rule — packet attestation is canonical; HEAD-specific on-disk status records are NOT required for preflight PASS), is required before any N-3 attempt.

**Deployed-runtime verification is still required before any N-3 attempt.** Post-commit deploy-and-verify cycle (per N-2r design):

**N-2s commit-time approval authorized only the four-file commit (`.nvmrc` + three status docs); it did NOT authorize a deploy (deploy is gate 5 per `orchestrator/APPROVAL-GATES.md`, separately approved), and it did NOT authorize N-3. N-2t commit-time approval authorizes only committing the runbook + three status docs; it does NOT authorize a deploy; it does NOT authorize N-3.**

- After the new HEAD is deployed to `agent-avila-dashboard` (auto-deploy or separately scoped deploy approval — gate 5 per `orchestrator/APPROVAL-GATES.md`, not authorized by any N-2z docs commit), the operator MUST verify, via Railway SSH into the deployed container after the redeploy reaches RUNNING:
  - The deployed commit SHA equals the new approved HEAD byte-for-byte (in-container `git rev-parse HEAD` if available — Case 1 per N-2o GAP-D — OR Railway dashboard "Deployed Commit" full 40-character SHA if `git` unavailable in container — Case 2).
  - In-container `node --version` matches `.nvmrc` byte-for-byte after parsing-hygiene normalization.
  - Deployed service is healthy (no crash loops, no rolling-deploy state).
- HALT if any of the above is false. Re-run an N-2r-preflight freshness check and update `.nvmrc` in a separately scoped phase before any N-3 attempt.

**N-2r-preflight freshness rule (per N-2r design).** The runtime-version capture used to set `.nvmrc` is invalidated by: an intervening deploy of `agent-avila-dashboard` between capture and the next N-3 attempt; a builder change (Nixpacks → Railpack or vice versa); a service / environment scope switch (non-production capture); inability to identify the running deployment SHA at capture time; or any Nixpacks Node provider version update that ships a new Node 24 patch. If freshness is broken, the operator MUST re-run in-container `node --version` capture in a fresh Railway SSH session and update `.nvmrc` before any N-3 attempt. Stale `.nvmrc` values that disagree with the deployed runtime would HALT N-3 at §4(x)(b); freshness violation is a maintenance issue, not a safety issue, but maintenance must precede the next N-3 attempt window.

**Active builder confirmed: Nixpacks v1.41.0** (Railway build log header). Nixpacks documents Node major-version control only; the deployed exact patch is determined at deploy time and is observable from in-container `node --version` (per the N-2r-preflight finding above).

**Approved primary N-3 execution surface confirmed: Railway SSH into the deployed `agent-avila-dashboard` production container.** N-2o GAP-A four conditions empirically PASS (surface available; running container; key setup OK; usable shell binary). **Railway local shell is NOT the approved surface** — Railway local shell opens a local subshell with Railway env vars injected (in-shell `node --version` returns the operator's local Node, not the deployed runtime), placing it in the same REJECTED category as Railway run (local-injection surface) (per runbook §3 N-2n rejection of local-injection surfaces).

**P2 informational item:** `package.json:8` declares `"start": "node bot.js"` but the deployed `agent-avila-dashboard` service is observed running `node dashboard.js` (per Railway build log). A service-side override (Railway custom start command, or Nixpacks-detected entry) reconciles the difference. Not material to Check D or the §11 N-3 gate; tracked here for future audit.

**Deploy-method source-identity gating (N-2t).** Operator-side fact-finding during a Path Z evaluation (N-3 against the currently-deployed HEAD via Option E without pushing N-2s) discovered that the currently-running production deploy of `agent-avila-dashboard` was triggered by `railway up` (the project's documented deploy command per `package.json` `scripts.deploy`) and exposes only deployment ID, image digest, build provenance (Nixpacks v1.41.0, `nodejs_24` setup), timestamp, and start command — but **no commit SHA on any non-secret Railway dashboard / build-log / deploy-metadata surface**. Both Cases of §4(x)(a) GAP-D failed for the currently-running deploy: Case 1 (in-container `git rev-parse HEAD`) returned `fatal: not a git repository` because the deployed NIXPACKS container has the `git` binary at `/bin/git` but no `.git/` metadata; Case 2 (Railway deploy-metadata fallback) returned no commit SHA because `railway up` deploys don't record one. **N-2t codifies the rule: deploys lacking a verifiable full 40-character commit SHA on a non-secret Railway deploy-metadata surface (e.g., `railway up` deploys) are REJECTED as a valid §4(x)(a) source-identity surface for N-3** — runbook §3 extended with deploy-method gating that mirrors the N-2n / N-2o rejection pattern; §4(x)(a) GAP-D Case 2 tightened to clarify image digest / deployment ID / timestamp / operator-attested mapping are NOT commit SHAs and do NOT satisfy the check. **The approved N-3 deploy path is GitHub-push-tracked deploys** (or any equivalent that produces a Railway-recorded commit SHA on a non-secret surface).

**Path Z block at HEAD `6c3a1e5` (informational record).** Operator chose Path Z (N-3 against currently-deployed HEAD via Option E) on 2026-05-04 to bypass the local DNS issue blocking the N-2s push. Path Z is currently STRUCTURALLY BLOCKED by the N-2t deploy-method rejection: the currently-running `railway up`-deployed instance does not satisfy §4(x)(a) GAP-D Case 2. To unblock, the operator must either (a) restore local DNS connectivity, push N-2s (`6c3a1e5`) to GitHub, allow Railway auto-deploy (or scoped manual deploy approval) to redeploy the new HEAD via GitHub-tracked deploy method (which records a commit SHA), and proceed with the post-commit deploy-and-verify cycle (Path X resumption); OR (b) use any equivalent deploy method that produces a Railway-recorded full 40-character commit SHA on a non-secret surface. **Path Z attempts via `railway up` are NOT a valid N-3 path under N-2t.**

**Migration 008 is APPLIED to production at HEAD `189eb1be6ef6304d914671bdaedec44d389cf877` (Attempt 6 — 2026-05-04, runner exit 0).** No further migration application, rollback, production DB query, deploy, Railway command, live Kraken action, env/secret read or write, `MANUAL_LIVE_ARMED` change, package/lockfile edit, runtime edit, or `position.json` change is authorized by this status. ## ANTIGRAVITY-MIGRATION-DESIGN-SPEC — Closed at `71af035f9a1f7489bfd663e099a15fda7439d0a7`

ANTIGRAVITY-MIGRATION-DESIGN-SPEC is the codification of the prior Codex-PASS conversation-only `ANTIGRAVITY-MIGRATION-DESIGN` (Mode 2) report as a SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/ANTIGRAVITY-MIGRATION-DESIGN.md`. The persisted design describes how Agent Avila's workflow can move into Google Antigravity IDE WITHOUT moving authority, secrets, commits, pushes, DB access, Railway access, Discord posting, Kraken API access, trading, Relay activation, Autopilot activation, or approvals. Architectural Option B chosen per DPI-A2: Antigravity = primary day-to-day safe-work surface (reading both repos, drafting design proposals, drafting future Phase F/G/H module code into scratch buffers, drafting tests, exploration/search, reading canonical handoff records, acting as additional pre-review brain analogous to ChatGPT fallback); Claude Code canonical session retains EXCLUSIVE control over all approvals, all secrets (Anthropic/Discord/Railway/Kraken/DB/GitHub tokens, .env), all staging/committing/pushing to both repos, all RED-tier actions (Railway/Discord/DB/Kraken/trading/Relay activation/Autopilot activation/external Hermes Agent integration/Stage 5-7/DASH-6/D-5.12f/Migration 009+/manual live-armed flag/position.json/memory-file writes/ARC-1-7 edits), and all Codex dispatches. All 10 DPI items resolved per operator answers: DPI-A1 Antigravity = Google Antigravity IDE; DPI-A2 Option B; DPI-A3 read access to agent-avila; DPI-A4 read access to agent-avila-relay; DPI-A5 NO memory-file access; DPI-A6 read-only CLI only (no npm install, no git push, no production DB, no node bot.js); DPI-A7 NO .env access; DPI-A8 Codex review required for all Antigravity-generated code before canonical placement; DPI-A9 no auto-memory sync; DPI-A10 no commits or pushes from Antigravity. Codex DESIGN-ONLY review track returned round-1 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS (1 RE applied verbatim conversation-only: RE-1 removed `git fetch` from §10 ALLOWED list and added it to FORBIDDEN list with conditional wording "git fetch from Antigravity unless separately approved in a future operator-manual setup phase" because `git fetch` mutates local Git metadata by updating refs/remotes/origin/*, downloading pack data, and writing to FETCH_HEAD — not strictly read-only); round-2 narrow re-review returned overall PASS across all 4 ultra-narrow goals with no new required edits. DOCS-ONLY (Mode 3) operator-directed codification phase. Scope: 4 parent-repo files (1 new SAFE-class handoff record + 3 status doc updates). Source-design HEAD `02edc238790c016fb5c36bc7b0fbdd563fa030f7` (= COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-DESIGN-SPEC). Authority preservation rules (firm; mirrors CLAUDE.md Discord-is-not-approval rule extended to Antigravity): Victor remains sole approver; Antigravity is NEVER an approval surface (no Antigravity reply, agent comment, code suggestion, autonomous run completion, IDE-side "approve" affordance, or any tool-side action constitutes operator approval); only Victor's in-session chat instruction in the canonical Claude Code session grants approval; Antigravity-hosted agents cannot self-approve (ROLE-HIERARCHY.md extended); no automation-non-promotion bypass (scheduled runs/cron/autonomous loops/background agents do NOT satisfy any operator-approval gate). Secret-handling rules (firm after DPI-A7): no .env access (workspace exclude + operator-side block; .env never opened/indexed/sent to Antigravity backend); no Railway/Discord/Kraken/DB/GitHub tokens ever in Antigravity context; no manual live-armed flag literal exposure in Antigravity-drafted text; git read access via SSH key only (git log/diff/status/show/ls-files allowed; git push and git commit to canonical branches forbidden per DPI-A10; git fetch forbidden per Codex RE-1); no memory-file mirroring per DPI-A5 (~/.claude/projects/.../memory/ stays scoped to Claude Code session); Antigravity's own credentials separate from Agent Avila credentials. Trading runtime boundary (firm): bot.js, dashboard.js, db.js, migrations/*, scripts/*, position.json* MUST NOT be edited from Antigravity, executed from Antigravity (no node bot.js per DPI-A6), have secrets accessed from Antigravity, or have production state read from Antigravity (no production DB connection per DPI-A6); per CLAUDE.md "do not modify live trading logic unless Victor explicitly approves" Antigravity inherits without exception; backtesting stays offline and separate from live trading; Strategy V2 cannot trade live during backtesting; Kraken API forbidden. Relay / external Hermes Agent boundary (firm): Relay stays DORMANT regardless of Antigravity setup; Antigravity may read Relay rules/runtime design/handoff records but cannot activate Relay or advance Stages 1-10b; external Hermes Agent (Nous/OpenRouter) NOT integrated via Antigravity; no Discord bot/token/webhook touch from Antigravity; future Phase G-GATEWAY drafting in Antigravity allowed as scratch-buffer drafting but Relay-repo commits follow operator-manual + Claude-Code-session pattern (no exceptions). Orchestrator boundary (firm): orchestrator/STATUS.md, CHECKLIST.md, NEXT-ACTION.md, all orchestrator/handoffs/*, all ARC-1 through ARC-7 safety-policy docs remain authoritatively maintained in the Claude Code session; Antigravity may READ these but NOT write them as source of truth; if a draft is composed in Antigravity, operator must paste back into Claude Code session; all phase opens, Codex dispatches, commits, pushes, closeouts initiated from canonical Claude Code session. Antigravity-generated code review rule (firm after DPI-A8): any code drafted in Antigravity that operator intends to bring into either repo MUST be carried back to Claude Code session by operator, staged for Codex on-disk review using same per-phase cadence as Phases C/D/E, optionally also Gemini review per 3-brains rule with ChatGPT fallback if Gemini quota fails, and only after Codex PASS may operator approve staging + commit + push from Claude Code session; applies to ALL code (src/, tests/, scripts/, migrations/, scaffolding files) with no exception. Memory-sync rule (firm after DPI-A9): no automatic memory cross-pollination; Antigravity does NOT read or write Claude Code auto-memory; no file-system memory bleed (workspace exclude rule for ~/.claude/projects/*/memory/); operator-mediated transfer only. CLI scope (firm; revised per Codex RE-1): ALLOWED = git status/log/diff/ls-files/show (read-only inspection only; git fetch removed per RE-1); cat/head/tail/less; grep/rg/ag/find; node --version / npm --version / node -e '<small-expr>'; hermetic test-suite invocation only if explicitly whitelisted by operator. FORBIDDEN = git push / git commit to canonical branches; git fetch from Antigravity unless separately approved in a future operator-manual setup phase (per Codex RE-1); npm install / ci / publish; node bot.js / dashboard.js / any trading code execution; psql / pg_dump / any DB client targeting production; railway / railway login/up/deploy; Discord CLI / curl to discord.com / webhook POSTs; Kraken API CLI; any command reading or writing .env; any command writing to ~/.claude/projects/*/memory/. Workspace configuration recommendations (operator-side; conversation-only): workspace exclude rules (.env, .env.*, **/node_modules/**, ~/.claude/**, position.json, position.json.snap.*, **/secrets/**, **/credentials/**, *.pem/*.key/*.p12/*.pfx); agent config disables agents that auto-invoke git push/git fetch/npm install/side-effecting CLI, disables MCP/extensions introducing new approval surfaces/schedulers/webhooks/background automation, disables cross-session memory/cloud-sync beyond Antigravity's own operation, disables auto-git-fetch on workspace open per Codex RE-1; repo access via read-only credentials only (SSH key or HTTPS read-only token; no GitHub PAT with repo:write; no SSH key with push privileges); operator workflow uses Antigravity for design/drafting/exploration and copies back to Claude Code session for canonical placement. Entire workspace-configuration section is conversation-only recommendation; no install step authorized by this DESIGN-SPEC. Pre-authorizes nothing downstream. Does NOT authorize: installing Google Antigravity IDE (operator-manual install only; separately gated); configuring any Antigravity workspace (separately gated); giving Antigravity any credential (no Anthropic/Discord/Railway/Kraken/DB/GitHub token; no SSH key with push privileges; no GitHub PAT with repo:write); any Antigravity-side commit/push/deploy/Discord/Railway/DB/Kraken/trading/Relay activation/Autopilot activation/external Hermes Agent integration; modification of ARC-1 through ARC-7; advancing phase-loop counter; breaking CEILING-PAUSE; modifying trading runtime; modifying orchestrator authority; posting to Discord; reading .env from Antigravity; reading auto-memory from Antigravity; running git push / git fetch / npm install / node bot.js / psql / railway from Antigravity; Stage 5 install resumption (Steps 14-21 remain deferred; Stage 5 Gate-10 install approval at `40f3137e842cd60acf1adf17ecc7fe2f0b1b8935` remains CONSUMED); Stage 7 dry-run; Stages 8/9/10a/10b; DASH-6 / D-5.12f / Migration 009+; memory-file edit; test-suite edit; modification of any other safety-policy doc / canonical Relay handoff record / runtime / migration / script / deploy file; Phase F SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (`COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT`) or any subsequent Relay-runtime lettered phase. ## ANTIGRAVITY-RULES-DESIGN-SPEC — Closed at `9d47f74d87aeed20a2fa7483a3704b494a21eb96`

ANTIGRAVITY-RULES-DESIGN-SPEC is the codification of the prior Codex-PASS conversation-only `ANTIGRAVITY-RULES-DESIGN` (Mode 2) report as a SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/ANTIGRAVITY-RULES-DESIGN.md` plus the actual canonical safety-rules file at `orchestrator/ANTIGRAVITY-RULES.md`. The persisted design defines an in-repo safety-rules file intended to be read by Antigravity-hosted AI agents (and any IDE-side AI agent that loads the workspace) when they first encounter the working tree. The file serves as a self-reminding agent-facing prompt that re-states the rules from CLAUDE.md + the canonical Antigravity migration design every time an Antigravity agent reads it. The file grants no new authority and is a derived restatement of existing canonical rules. Stage 16 enters pipeline loop); `src/runtime/run-pipeline.js` (286 LOC; 11-gate per-message orchestrator per §6; post-gate-11 routing: dry-run → `dryRunLog.append` + `moveToProcessed`; production → `phaseGSendAndRecord` callable per RE-8); `src/runtime/halt.js` (228 LOC; halt-state machine per §5: `extractHaltClass` 5-step strict precedence with `typeof === 'number'` guards per RE-3 + RE-C; `executePerMessageHalt` append-then-move with RE-4 failure path); `src/runtime/pipeline-state.js` (35 LOC; `{ substitutedBody }` accumulator); `src/runtime/rate-limit-state.js` (183 LOC; Phase F-owned counter keyed by `(operatorPhaseId, channelName)` per RE-9 with restart-no-reset semantics; fails closed if `operatorPhaseId` missing); `src/runtime/class-auth-counter.js` (55 LOC; Phase F-owned class-auth use counter for gate 4 with `null` return for non-string keys per Phase E hardening); `src/runtime/single-instance-lock.js` (227 LOC; atomic `mkdir` lock under `validatedEnv.MESSAGE_STORE_PATH` per RE-10 + RE-A; POSIX `kill(pid,0)` liveness probe with no `lsof` primary; halt class 9 on contested acquisition or staleness unprovable; stale-lock recovery via PID liveness probe); `src/runtime/safe-log-binding.js` (36 LOC; pre-bound 2-arg `safeLog(level, payload)` wrapper around Phase C 3-arg `safeLog`); `src/runtime/boot-halt-fallback.js` (61 LOC; pre-logger Tier-1 synchronous stdout JSON fallback per RE-B with strict `{event, haltClass, reason}` payload only); `src/runtime/index.js` (43 LOC; optional pure re-export aggregator per DPI-F9 mirroring Phase E `src/verify/index.js` pattern). ## COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-AMENDMENT-DESIGN-SPEC — Closed at `f7d511c31f36b6d39b2b7cfe79cba9c8e31d10ee`

COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-AMENDMENT-DESIGN-SPEC is the codification of the prior conversation-only `COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-AMENDMENT-DESIGN` (Mode 2 / DESIGN-ONLY) v5 report as a SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-AMENDMENT-DESIGN.md`. The persisted design covers a future SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4) phase `COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-AMENDMENT` whose scope is exactly 1 Relay-repo file (`src/runtime/boot.js`), 5 hunks, ~30 lines, fixing four confirmed Phase F interface-contract drifts between `boot.js` and the sealed Phase C/D/E factories it consumes: (Bug 1) `validateEnv` returns `{ env }` but `boot.js:139-142` destructures `{ parsed }` causing fail-loud TypeError pre-Stage 2; (Bug 2) `createOperatorAuthorizationGate` (sealed Phase E factory at `operator-authorization.js:72-76`) requires `stalenessThresholdMs` but `boot.js:374-381` (Stage 12) + `boot.js:481-488` (Stage 14) pass ignored `classAuthorizations` causing fail-silent per-message staleness bypass with class-auth path unaffected; (Bug 3) `createLimitsGate` (sealed Phase E factory at `limits.js:56-61`) requires `channelRateLimits` dereferenced at `limits.js:79` but `boot.js:401-408` (Stage 12) + `boot.js:489-496` (Stage 14) omit it causing fail-loud TypeError on per-message `verify()` execution; (Bug 4) `limits.js:47-50,81-82` consumer documents/consumes `getRateLimitState()` → `{ channelName: count }` map but sealed Phase D producer `rate-limit-state.js:5,116-157` implements `getRateLimitState({ channelName }) → { withinLimit, count, limit, windowMs, operatorPhaseId }` status object, and `boot.js:494` directly pass-throughs the producer without an adapter, causing fail-silent rate-limit bypass (halt class 5 `RATE_LIMIT_HIT` never fires regardless of message volume). All four bugs were independently confirmed via READ-ONLY AUDITs against sealed source. Codex DESIGN-ONLY rounds 3 + 4 + 5 history: round-3 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS at Goal 17 surfacing Bug 4 (callable-shape drift) + Goal 19 flagging parent-HEAD anchor mismatch → expanded v2 → v3 → v4; round-4 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS at Goal 11 requiring two documentation corrections to §12 + §14 halt-class numbering → applied to v5; round-5 first dispatch mixed (substantive PASS on halt-class numbering verified against sealed `HALT_CLASS = Object.freeze({...})` constants in `limits.js:25-28`, `operator-authorization.js:38-41`, `boot.js:105-116`, `config.js:28-31`, `rate-limit-state.js:38-40`; procedural FAIL because v5 was intentionally conversation-only with no persisted file at the time of review); round-5 clarification resume overall PASS (procedural FAIL withdrawn after recognizing conversation-only mode; Changes A–E confirmed structurally sound and unchanged from v4; parent HEAD discrepancy confirmed reconciled). Five changes covered verbatim: Change A Stage 1 validateEnv destructure (2 lines); Change B Stage 12 createOperatorAuthorizationGate with `stalenessThresholdMs` from `runtimeConfig.operatorAuthorizationStalenessThresholdMs` + 24h canonical fallback (~5 lines); Change C Stage 14 createOperatorAuthorizationGate re-bind mirror of Change B with `classAuthCounter.getClassAuthorizationUseCount` wired (~5 lines); Change D Stage 12 createLimitsGate adds `channelRateLimits` from `runtimeConfig.channelRateLimits || {}` (~4 lines) mirroring existing pattern at `boot.js:446-448`; Change E Stage 14 createLimitsGate re-bind adds `channelRateLimits` + `getRateLimitState` adapter closure that iterates `Object.keys(runtimeConfig.channelRateLimits || {})` and calls producer's `rateLimitState.getRateLimitState({ channelName })` per configured channel extracting `.count` to build the consumer's expected channel→count map (~14 lines). Adapter is sync with no fs/network/env reads; consumer's await is harmless for sync return; closure captures `runtimeConfig` (declared `boot.js:127-133`) and `rateLimitState` (declared `boot.js:442-450`) from Stage 14 scope. Halt-class wiring unchanged; restores correct behavior for already-defined canonical §15 IDs 2 (`OPERATOR_AUTHORIZATION_MISSING_OR_INVALID` at `operator-authorization.js:39`), 4 (`CHARACTER_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` at `limits.js:26`), 5 (`RATE_LIMIT_HIT` at `limits.js:27`), 10 (`CLASS_AUTHORIZATION_BOUNDS_VIOLATION` at `operator-authorization.js:40`), 20 (`CONFIG_FORBIDDEN_ENV_VAR_PRESENT` at `config.js:29` / `ENV_INVALID` at `boot.js:108` / `CONFIG_INVALID` at `rate-limit-state.js:39` — three local aliases for canonical §15 ID 20), 21 (`CONFIG_REQUIRED_ENV_VAR_MISSING` at `config.js:30` / `ENV_MISSING_REQUIRED` at `boot.js:109` — two local aliases for canonical §15 ID 21). Per-module HALT_CLASS Object.freeze pattern preserved per DPI-F-IMPL-4 carry-forward (each module declares only canonical §15 IDs it consumes; no `HaltClass` import from Phase C). Bug 3 / Bug 4 sequencing: Bug 3 crashes at `limits.js:79` before Bug 4 path at `limits.js:81-82` is reachable, so Bug 4 was only observable post-Bug-3-fix; the amendment fixes both in the same commit so neither manifests in production after landing. Parent HEAD reconciliation completed via READ-ONLY AUDIT: session-start gitStatus `51662f3…` was an ancestor 18 commits behind current `4a0e5518…` on `main`; no commits lost; stale snapshot. ## PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN-SPEC — Closed at `c8798ea94b1b7cf55a589a253e99e5d5476178de`

PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN-SPEC is the codification of the prior conversation-only `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN` (Mode 2 / DESIGN-ONLY) v2 report as a SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN.md`. The persisted implementation design describes how the future SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4) phase `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT` will author a read-only Node generator script (recommended path `tools/dashboard-generate.js`, subject to Codex implementation review at Mode 4 implementation time) plus an initial `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` consistent with the canonical dashboard DESIGN handoff at `f6aaa40…` (sealed; not modified by this codification). Generator behavior: stdout-only, never writes files at runtime; operator pipes via `>` and explicitly stages the result during separately-gated regeneration phases. Hard guarantees: no write-side fs APIs imported; no network module imports (no `http`/`https`/`fetch`/`net`/`tls`/`dgram`); no `child_process` invocation outside the 4 allowed read-only git commands; no `process.env` reads at all — the future generator must not read environment variables. Three internal layers: data layer (`fs.readFileSync` against explicit path allowlist + 4 read-only git commands via `child_process.execFileSync` against explicit command allowlist); parse layer (regex-based section detection; checklist `[x]`/`[ ]` counting); render layer (assembles 11 content sections + header metadata to stdout). Allowed local read paths (12): `orchestrator/STATUS.md`, `orchestrator/CHECKLIST.md`, `orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION.md`, `orchestrator/handoffs/*.md`, `orchestrator/AUTOPILOT-RULES.md`, `orchestrator/APPROVAL-GATES.md`, `orchestrator/PHASE-MODES.md`, `orchestrator/PROTECTED-FILES.md`, `orchestrator/COMM-HUB-RULES.md`, `orchestrator/COMM-HUB-RELAY-RULES.md`, `orchestrator/COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-DESIGN.md`, `CLAUDE.md`. Refused/forbidden paths (deny-by-default allowlist): `bot.js`, `dashboard.js`, `db.js`, `migrations/*`, `scripts/*`, `package*.json`, `position.json*`, `.env*`, `.nvmrc`, `railway.json`, secrets, Relay repo paths, memory files, test files, exchange paths, production DB clients/queries, Discord bot/token/webhook/scheduler surfaces, Claude settings/hooks/MCP/slash-command permission files, Railway/DB/external API endpoints. Allowed git commands (4 only): `git log --oneline -N`, `git rev-parse HEAD`, `git rev-parse origin/main`, `git status --short`. Forbidden git commands: `git ls-remote` (network), `git fetch`/`pull`/`push` (network/write), `git checkout`/`reset`/`merge`/`rebase` (write), `git commit`/`add`/`tag`/`stash` (write). Parse-failure behavior: fail loud (exit non-zero with structured stderr error); no partial dashboard emission; `--partial` flag explicitly out of scope for v1. Initial `DASHBOARD.md` output structure: header metadata (4 fields: Generated ISO-8601 timestamp; Parent HEAD short SHA + repo name; Relay HEAD short SHA + repo name + sealed status; working-tree state) plus 11 content sections per canonical DESIGN §9 (Where Are We Now; Active Phase; Safety Gates; Completed Phases; Paused Phases; Pending/Future Designed Phases; Backlog/Future Ideas; Phase Timeline/Roadmap; Repo Anchors; Dormant vs Active Systems with `bot.js`/`dashboard.js` labeled OFF-SCOPE/NOT ASSESSED BY THIS DASHBOARD per canonical RE-1/RE-2; Next Safe Action); approximate length 80-120 lines; canonical DESIGN §4 is a 10-row summary and is not the authoritative section count. 5-file future implementation scope: NEW `tools/dashboard-generate.js` (~150-250 LOC Node ES modules, Node built-ins only) + NEW `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` (~80-120 lines initial output) + MODIFIED `orchestrator/STATUS.md` + MODIFIED `orchestrator/CHECKLIST.md` + MODIFIED `orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION.md`. Test scope: no new test files in v1 implementation scope; existing validators may be run per CLAUDE.md §Change discipline and PHASE-MODES.md Mode 4; `npm install`/`npm ci` remains out of scope unless separately gated; a separate `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-TESTS-DESIGN` phase can design new unit tests later if desired. Codex DESIGN-ONLY review history: round-1 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS — 4 required edits applied verbatim (RE-1 script path qualification subject to Mode 4 Codex review; RE-2 zero `process.env` reads at all; RE-3 test-scope justification corrected per CLAUDE.md §Change discipline + PHASE-MODES.md Mode 4; RE-4 canonical §9 cited as 11-content-section authority with canonical §4 noted as 10-row summary); round-2 narrow re-review overall PASS across all 13 narrow goals with no further required edits, no structural design changes, 5-file scope unchanged, stdout-only behavior unchanged, zero-network policy unchanged, no new forbidden literals, Mode 2 design classification preserved, future Mode 4 implementation classification preserved. DOCS-ONLY (Mode 3) codification phase. Scope: 4 parent-repo files (1 new SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN.md` + 3 status doc updates `orchestrator/STATUS.md`, `orchestrator/CHECKLIST.md`, `orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION.md`). Source-design HEAD `19db4679aa32fe2e341f86acbedd1f79dc703ecd` (= prior CLOSEOUT-SYNC commit). Pre-authorizes nothing downstream. Does NOT authorize: opening `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT` (SAFE IMPLEMENTATION / Mode 4 — separately gated); creating generator code; creating `tools/` directory; creating `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md`; opening any future dashboard implementation cascade phase or `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-TESTS-DESIGN`; opening `COMM-HUB-RELAY-RUNTIME-IMPLEMENT-F-HALT-AMENDMENT`; opening Phase G; activating Relay; running smoke tests against Phase F; Stage 5 install resumption; Stage 7 dry-run; Stages 8/9/10a/10b; deploying any Relay runtime; any platform-side application/bot/token/permission/webhook/post action; any deploy-command invocation; any database-client invocation; any exchange API call; any env-variable or secret change; manual live-armed flag action; DASH-6 / D-5.12f / Migration 009+; Autopilot Loop B/C/D activation; CEILING-PAUSE break; external Hermes Agent (Nous/OpenRouter); memory-file edit; test-suite edit; modification of any safety-policy doc / canonical Relay handoff record / runtime / migration / script / deploy file; `npm install`/`npm ci`; modification of Phase C/D/E/F sealed files; modification of `package.json`/`package-lock.json`; Antigravity install change / workspace reconfiguration / `orchestrator/ANTIGRAVITY-RULES.md` content edit / Relay-side parallel `ANTIGRAVITY-RULES.md` placement; cleanup of inherited forbidden-content literals (separately gated); any network lookup; scheduler / cron / webhook / MCP install; permission widening. ## PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT — Closed at `f5cc97af98cd0e2303fbc2ca401a0c9bb2c05d36`

PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT is the SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4) phase authoring the read-only Project Progress Dashboard generator per the sealed canonical handoffs at `orchestrator/handoffs/PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-DESIGN.md` (codified at `f6aaa40…`) and `orchestrator/handoffs/PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN.md` (codified at `c8798ea…`). 5-file scope: NEW `tools/dashboard-generate.js` (~400 LOC Node ES modules, Node built-ins only — `fs.readFileSync`, `path`, `child_process.execFileSync`, `url`); NEW `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` (~126 lines initial output captured via operator-approved `node tools/dashboard-generate.js > orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` redirection — the script emits to stdout and never writes files itself); MODIFIED `orchestrator/STATUS.md`, `orchestrator/CHECKLIST.md`, `orchestrator/NEXT-ACTION.md`. Source-design HEAD `7b7e1cc771ba84075426a36926b2a855fbd06cbc` (= prior PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN-SPEC-CLOSEOUT-SYNC commit). Generator behavior: stdout-only Node script invoked as `node tools/dashboard-generate.js` (zero args); reads canonical orchestrator markdown via `fs.readFileSync` against an explicit 12-item path allowlist + glob `orchestrator/handoffs/*.md`; runs only 4 read-only git commands via `execFileSync` against an explicit command allowlist (`git log --oneline -N`, `git rev-parse HEAD`, `git rev-parse origin/main`, `git status --short`); parses STATUS.md section headers via regex (`## PHASE — IN PROGRESS` / `## PHASE — Closed at \`SHA\``); renders 11 content sections + header metadata; fail-loud on any allowlist or parse failure (exit non-zero with stderr `DashboardError: <reason> at <context>`); no partial dashboard emission. Hard guarantees verified by code: no `fs.writeFile*`/`appendFile*`/`createWriteStream`/`mkdir*`/`unlink*`/`rename*`/`rm*` imported; no `http`/`https`/`fetch`/`net`/`tls`/`dgram` imported; no `child_process.exec`/`spawn`/`execFile` beyond the 4 allowed git commands; no `process.env` reads anywhere in the code. Initial `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` (126 lines) contains: header metadata (Generated ISO-8601 timestamp; Parent HEAD `7b7e1cc` in sync with origin/main; Relay HEAD `b8ab035` Phase F sealed; working-tree note); Where Are We Now (1 sentence); Active Phase block; Safety Gates table (10 canonical gates: Relay DORMANT, Autopilot DORMANT, Discord posting NOT ACTIVE, live trading NOT AUTHORIZED, manual live-armed flag OPERATOR-ONLY, approvers {Victor}, CEILING-PAUSE broken via ARC-8-UNPAUSE counter 0 of 3, Migration 008 APPLIED, Stage 5 Gate-10 install CONSUMED, N-3 deploy gate CLOSED); Completed Phases table (26 entries parsed from STATUS.md, most recent first); Paused Phases (F-HALT-SMOKE-DESIGN round-1 4 REs pending); Designed/Not-Opened (F-HALT-AMENDMENT Mode 4, F-HALT-AMENDMENT-CLOSEOUT, PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-IMPLEMENT Mode 4, Phase G Mode 5); Backlog/Future Ideas (14 items per canonical DESIGN §5); Phase Timeline/Roadmap (ASCII gantt; committed-anchored only); Repo Anchors (Parent + Relay); Dormant vs Active Systems table with `bot.js` and `dashboard.js` labeled OFF-SCOPE / NOT ASSESSED BY THIS DASHBOARD per canonical RE-1/RE-2; Next Safe Action (1 sentence). Codex DESIGN-ONLY review history (source design phase): IMPLEMENT-DESIGN v2 round-2 PASS after round-1 4 REs applied; IMPLEMENT-DESIGN-SPEC round-1 PASS at `c8798ea…` no REs; IMPLEMENT-DESIGN-SPEC-CLOSEOUT round-1 PASS at `dceba5b…` no REs; IMPLEMENT-DESIGN-SPEC-CLOSEOUT-SYNC round-1 PASS at `7b7e1cc…` no REs. SAFE IMPLEMENTATION (Mode 4) phase. Scope: 5 parent-repo files (2 new + 3 modified). Pre-authorizes nothing downstream. ## PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-DESIGN-SPEC — Closed at `1b49fc30737ea96ec8d2dbf923c5467eb33b8149`

PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-DESIGN-SPEC is the codification of the prior conversation-only `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-DESIGN` (Mode 2 / DESIGN-ONLY) v2 report as a SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-DESIGN.md`. The persisted design covers a future local-only static futuristic website (Astro + Tailwind recommended; vanilla / 11ty fallback if dep footprint judged unacceptable at SCAFFOLD review) that renders the existing `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` as a richer visual layer — entirely separate from the trading runtime `dashboard.js` and the trading dashboard surface. Future cascade is 11 phases (DESIGN → DESIGN-SPEC → DESIGN-SPEC-CLOSEOUT → DESIGN-SPEC-CLOSEOUT-SYNC → IMPLEMENT-DESIGN → IMPLEMENT-DESIGN-SPEC → IMPLEMENT-DESIGN-SPEC-CLOSEOUT → IMPLEMENT-DESIGN-SPEC-CLOSEOUT-SYNC → SCAFFOLD → SCAFFOLD-CLOSEOUT+SYNC → CONTENT) with SCAFFOLD as the first Mode 4 phase introducing `web/package.json` + `web/package-lock.json` (isolated from root); CONTENT as the second Mode 4 phase adding components + DASHBOARD.md parsing. Local-only static architecture: no backend; no SSR; no deploy; no runtime network; no service worker with background sync; no telemetry; vendored fonts/icons; build operator-run only. Data source for v1: `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` at build time (no modification of sealed generator `tools/dashboard-generate.js` at `f5cc97a…`); future JSON export deferred to a separately gated `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-JSON-EXPORT` phase. Futuristic visual theme: cyberpunk control-room aesthetic; dark background; cyan/violet/emerald/amber/coral palette; JetBrains Mono + Inter typography; glass-morphism cards; status pills with optional pulse glow; traffic-light safety gates; vertical phase timeline with SHA chips; respect `prefers-reduced-motion`; WCAG AA contrast; keyboard navigation; `prefers-color-scheme` honored. 12 website sections mirror DASHBOARD.md with richer presentation (Header / Hero / Active Phase / Safety Gates / Completed Phases / Paused / Designed-Not-Opened / Backlog filterable / Phase Timeline / Repo Anchors / Dormant-vs-Active with `bot.js` and `dashboard.js` clearly marked OFF-SCOPE / NOT ASSESSED BY THIS DASHBOARD / Next Safe Action) plus a permanent footer disclaimer that the website is not the trading dashboard. Future file scope: ~30-50 new files inside `web/` (isolated package.json + lockfile; gitignored node_modules + dist) + 3 modified parent status docs per phase; root `package.json` and `package-lock.json` are RESTRICTED per `PROTECTED-FILES.md`, unrelated to the web project, and must remain untouched; web dependencies, if ever authorized, live only under `web/package.json` and `web/package-lock.json`; sealed generator at `f5cc97a…` and refreshed dashboard at `eb0634f…` both remain untouched; both canonical PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD handoffs at `f6aaa40…` and `c8798ea…` remain sealed. Forbidden files (deny-by-default at build + runtime): `bot.js`, `dashboard.js`, `db.js`, `position.json*`, `.env*`, secrets, `.nvmrc`, `migrations/*`, `scripts/*`, root `package*.json`, `railway.json`, deploy scripts, CI/CD config, live Kraken/exchange paths, production DB clients/queries, Discord bot/token/webhook/scheduler surfaces, Relay repo paths, memory files, test files. 10 safety invariants enforced. 10 permanent trading-dashboard separation invariants (naming disambiguation, file-path isolation, trading-data forbidden surface, no port-sharing, no CLI confusion, no code import boundary crossing, no shared `node_modules`, canonical handoff disclaimer, footer disclaimer on every page, Codex review pattern with explicit trading-isolation goal at every Mode 4 phase). PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN-SPEC is the codification of the prior conversation-only `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN` (Mode 2 / DESIGN-ONLY) v4 Codex-clean report as a SAFE-class handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN.md`. The 19-section codified design preserves: §0 verifiability framing (file counts, LOC, pins, checklist counts are design targets confirmed at future Codex SCAFFOLD / CONTENT reviews against git-state evidence); §1 Mode 2 / DESIGN-ONLY classification of the source design + Mode 3 / DOCS-ONLY classification of this codification phase; §2 SCAFFOLD scope with six explicit constraints (no real data; no parser; no runtime controls; no DASHBOARD.md content; no fake phase data; first-screen disclaimer copy present per §13 Goal 18); §3 CONTENT sub-scope table; §4 Astro + Tailwind dependency plan with Tailwind adapter review gate at SCAFFOLD (drop if plain Tailwind/PostCSS suffices); §5 version pinning + lockfile integrity verbatim sequencing (first install creates lockfile; thereafter `npm ci` only; root package files + workspace fields untouched; lockfile integrity reviewed before any build); §6 Antigravity policy verbatim rule (no SCAFFOLD assistance at all; CONTENT visual-polish-only in component files; never edit parser/data/config/package files; no commit/push authority); §7 web/ isolation 8 layers (directory boundary + distinct `node_modules` + no workspaces + no symlinks + distinct lockfiles + gitignore isolation + code-import boundary + no process/env/network coupling); §8 DASHBOARD.md build-time read model with parser purity (no fs / no shell / no network / no `Date.now()` / no random; fail-loud `DashboardLoadError` / `DashboardParseError`; single allowlisted path; `load-dashboard.ts` is the only file with `fs.*`); §9 forbidden-file protection 8 enforcement layers + forbidden-literal scan in prose-neutral form (exact patterns reserved for narrowly scoped Codex prompt criteria); §10 no backend / no SSR / no deploy / no network at 5 layers + static-only `output: 'static'`; §11 first scaffold visual design with v1 `system-ui` + `ui-monospace` font stacks (vendored fonts deferred), hero paraphrase paragraph (`"This dashboard summarizes orchestrator state at build time. ## PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-SCAFFOLD — Closed at `cc6819d46689e448911cac2c200113cd76670c74`

PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-SCAFFOLD is the **first Mode 4 SAFE IMPLEMENTATION phase in the WEB cascade**, recorded retrospectively here. It created the initial isolated local-only static futuristic Astro + Tailwind scaffold under `web/` per the sealed WEB-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN handoff at `e6af54a91a94cc4b92291a550db3825a8bb599a5`. Operator-approved sub-decisions at SCAFFOLD time: (B) drop `@astrojs/tailwind` — use plain Tailwind via `postcss.config.mjs` with 4 deps total (astro, tailwindcss, autoprefixer, postcss); (C) write `web/package.json` with placeholder caret-range version targets, exact patch pinning resolved by the separately approved first `npm install` step and recorded in `web/package-lock.json`; (E) write the 13 scaffold source/config/docs files first, then run the single authorized `npm install` after a separate operator approval. The 14-file SCAFFOLD scope committed at `cc6819d…` and pushed as fast-forward `831af05..cc6819d` to `relentlessvic/agent-avila` `origin/main` with three-way SHA consistency PASS verified post-push at local HEAD = origin/main = live remote `refs/heads/main` = `cc6819d46689e448911cac2c200113cd76670c74`. The 14 committed files: `web/.gitignore`, `web/package.json` (4 deps; `"private": true`; no `workspaces`; placeholder caret ranges), `web/package-lock.json` (lockfileVersion 3; 448 nm entries; integrity hashes on all entries; resolved versions astro 5.18.1 / tailwindcss 3.4.19 / autoprefixer 10.5.0 / postcss 8.5.14), `web/astro.config.mjs` (`output: 'static'`; no SSR adapter; no API routes; no middleware), `web/tailwind.config.mjs` (cyber palette + `system-ui` + `ui-monospace` font stacks per RE-7), `web/postcss.config.mjs` (Tailwind + autoprefixer plugins), `web/tsconfig.json` (extends `astro/tsconfigs/strict`), `web/src/styles/global.css` (`@tailwind` directives + CSS custom props + `prefers-reduced-motion` rules + skip-link), `web/src/styles/theme.css` (glass-morphism + status-pill + neon-divider + pulse-glow animation gated behind `prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference`), `web/src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` (semantic landmarks; skip-link; header with "AGENT AVILA / PROGRESS CONSOLE" + "STATUS: SCAFFOLD READY" pill; footer with canonical verbatim disclaimer "This web project is entirely separate from the trading runtime `dashboard.js`. It does not modify, replace, or supplement live trading functionality."), `web/src/pages/index.astro` (empty futuristic shell with hero paraphrase paragraph "This dashboard summarizes orchestrator state at build time. It is entirely separate from the trading runtime. Do not infer trading state from this UI." — no parser invocation; no DASHBOARD.md content; no fake phase data; no runtime controls), `web/public/favicon.svg` (inline vector glyph; vendored only; no external URL), `web/public/.gitkeep` (reserves public dir; no vendored font files at v1 per RE-7), `web/README.md` (local-only build instructions; canonical trading-isolation disclaimer; `system-ui` / `ui-monospace` font note; Mode 4 cascade reference). The `web/node_modules/` directory was created by the operator-approved first `npm install` (165M, 290 packages at depth 2, 448 total nm entries) and is correctly gitignored via `web/.gitignore` (no node_modules in commit). Codex SAFE IMPLEMENTATION review history: round-1 PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS — 1 cosmetic neutralization RE (bare `child_process` literal in `web/README.md:31` replaced with "No Node shell-process calls anywhere in web/"); round-2 narrow re-review PASS across all 15 narrow goals with no further required edits; post-install lockfile review PASS across all 22 review goals with no required edits. **Audit advisory carried forward (operator-aware; remediation deferred per design §5 rule 7):** 1 moderate severity advisory in astro 5.18.1 ("XSS in `define:vars` via incomplete `</script>` tag sanitization"; affected range `<6.1.6`). Scaffold scan confirms the vulnerable `define:vars` feature is NOT used anywhere in `web/src/**` or `web/*.mjs`/`*.json`. Per the sealed design §5 rule 7, audit findings are advisories; remediation goes through a separately gated future phase (would require bumping astro to a v6.x major version). No `npm audit fix --force` was run. No `web/dist/` build output; no `web/.astro/` cache; no operator-only runtime commands ran (no `npm run dev` / `npm run build` / `npm run preview` / `npx`). The 8 isolation layers from the WEB-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN §7 are intact: web/ is a sibling directory with its own `node_modules`, its own lockfile, its own gitignore; no workspaces declaration in root `package.json`; no symlinks; no cross-boundary imports; no `process.env.*` reads in web/src/**; no `child_process` calls anywhere in web/; no `fs.*` calls in web/src/** (the parser lands in CONTENT, not SCAFFOLD); no build-time shell SHA injection. RE-2 SCAFFOLD constraints fully honored: no real data; no parser; no runtime controls; no DASHBOARD.md content; no fake phase data; first-screen canonical disclaimer copy verbatim in footer + hero paraphrase per §13 Goal 18.

## PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-CONTENT — Closed at `04f7b7fa5d6c9e0b3097fa2353747aa376e77860`

PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-WEB-CONTENT is the **second Mode 4 SAFE IMPLEMENTATION phase in the WEB cascade**, recorded retrospectively here. It added the real Project Progress Dashboard content layer on top of the SCAFFOLD shell per the sealed WEB-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN handoff at `e6af54a91a94cc4b92291a550db3825a8bb599a5`. Operator-approved sub-decisions: Q1 (a) static backlog list at v1 — no client-side filter UI; Q2 (a) keep both headers — BaseLayout chrome header AND content Header.astro; Q3 (a) exact WEB-DESIGN §5 section order. The 26-file CONTENT scope was committed at `04f7b7f…` and pushed as fast-forward `1aeb503..04f7b7f` to `relentlessvic/agent-avila` `origin/main` with three-way SHA consistency PASS verified post-push at local HEAD = origin/main = live remote `refs/heads/main` = `04f7b7fa5d6c9e0b3097fa2353747aa376e77860`. Delivered scope: 26 files total (946 insertions, 15 deletions) — 24 new files (5 parser/data layer in `web/src/lib/`: types, constants, format, dashboard-parser, load-dashboard; 6 UI primitives in `web/src/components/`: Card, Pill, SHAChip, Section, Disclaimer, Icon; 12 section components in `web/src/components/sections/`: Header, Hero, ActivePhase, SafetyGates, CompletedPhases, PausedPhases, DesignedNotOpened, Backlog, PhaseTimeline, RepoAnchors, DormantVsActive, NextSafeAction; 1 animation CSS file at `web/src/styles/animations.css`) + 2 modified files (`web/src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` imports animations.css and uses Disclaimer in footer; `web/src/pages/index.astro` replaced scaffold hero with full 12-section render of parsed `DashboardData`). Operator's plan was 27 files (24 new + 3 modifications); actual delivery is 26 files (24 new + 2 modifications) because `web/tailwind.config.mjs` was NOT modified — Tailwind 3 handles opacity modifiers (`bg-cyber-cyan/10`, `border-cyber-cyan/35`, etc.) automatically against the existing custom `cyber.*` palette so no config change was needed. The static site can now render real orchestrator DASHBOARD.md data (parent HEAD, 43 closed phases at REFRESH-003 snapshot anchor `1839524…`, 10 safety gates, 14 backlog items, phase timeline, etc.) when an operator separately authorizes `npm run build` — but build/preview/dev/deploy did NOT run during this phase. Codex SAFE IMPLEMENTATION review history: round-1 source-files review returned FAIL with 3 substantive REs (RE-1 parser fail-loud: `parseCompletedPhases` / `parseBacklog` / `parseRepoAnchors` / `parseDormantVsActive` now throw `DashboardParseError` on empty result while `parsePausedPhases` / `parseDesignedNotOpened` remain empty-allowed; RE-2 accessible headings: `Section.astro` now sets `aria-labelledby={\`${id}-heading\`}` on `<section>` and `id={\`${id}-heading\`}` on `<h2>`; RE-3 use Card/Disclaimer/Icon: all 12 section components import and use `<Card>` instead of inline `glass-card` divs, `Disclaimer.astro` refactored to accept slot and used in BaseLayout footer with inline `<code>dashboard.js</code>`, `Section.astro` accepts optional `icon` prop rendered via `<Icon>` and 5 sections pass icon names — ActivePhase=lightning, SafetyGates=shield, NextSafeAction=arrow, PausedPhases=pause, CompletedPhases=check); all 3 REs applied verbatim. Codex SAFE IMPLEMENTATION round-2 returned FAIL only on Goal K `child_process` scan scope artefact (Codex's broad scan claimed third-party matches in node_modules-internal code; first-party scan `grep -rln 'child_process' web/src web/*.mjs` returned 0 matches). Codex SAFE IMPLEMENTATION round-3 narrow re-review with explicit scope clarification returned PASS across all 12 narrow goals (first-party zero-match confirmed; `web/.gitignore` properly excludes `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `.astro/`; sealed §9 rule 4 boundary interpretation accepted — first-party `web/src/**` + `web/*.mjs` is in scope, gitignored third-party `node_modules/` is out of scope). 8 isolation layers preserved: directory boundary, distinct `node_modules`, no workspaces in root, no symlinks, distinct lockfiles, gitignore isolation, code-import boundary, no process/env/network coupling. Parser purity preserved: no `fs.*`, no `child_process`, no network, no `Date.now()` / `new Date()`, no `Math.random()` / `crypto.randomUUID()` in `dashboard-parser.ts`. `load-dashboard.ts` remains the sole `fs.*` source in `web/src/**`. **Audit advisory carried forward (operator-aware; remediation deferred per design §5 rule 7):** 1 moderate severity in astro 5.18.1 (`define:vars` XSS; affected `<6.1.6`). Scan confirms the vulnerable `define:vars` feature is NOT used anywhere in `web/src/**` or `web/*.mjs`/`*.json`. No `npm install` / `npm ci` / `npx` / `astro dev` / `astro preview` / `astro build` / `npm audit fix` ran during this phase. No new npm dependencies. Root `package.json` + `package-lock.json` RESTRICTED and untouched. Sealed generator `tools/dashboard-generate.js` at `f5cc97a…` untouched. Refreshed `orchestrator/DASHBOARD.md` at REFRESH-003 / `738febe…` untouched. WEB-DESIGN handoff sealed at `1b49fc3…` untouched. WEB-IMPLEMENT-DESIGN handoff sealed at `e6af54a…` untouched. Canonical prior PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD handoffs at `f6aaa40…` and `c8798ea…` sealed and untouched. RE-2 SCAFFOLD constraints preserved (no real data outside parser, no fake data, no runtime controls). Canonical footer disclaimer verbatim in `BaseLayout.astro`: "This web project is entirely separate from the trading runtime `dashboard.js`. It does not modify, replace, or supplement live trading functionality." (now rendered via `<Disclaimer>` slot). Antigravity not used. **The web/ directory now contains 38 tracked files: 14 SCAFFOLD files from `cc6819d…` + 24 CONTENT files from `04f7b7f…` + 2 SCAFFOLD files modified at `04f7b7f…` (BaseLayout.astro + index.astro).** Future build-and-preview remains separately gated and NOT authorized by this CLOSEOUT.

## PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-UX-UPGRADE-DESIGN-SPEC — IN PROGRESS

PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-UX-UPGRADE-DESIGN-SPEC is a DOCS-ONLY (Mode 3) operator-directed codification phase persisting the accepted conversation-only `PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-UX-UPGRADE-DESIGN` (DESIGN-ONLY / Mode 2) as an on-disk handoff record at `orchestrator/handoffs/PROJECT-PROGRESS-DASHBOARD-UX-UPGRADE-DESIGN.md`. The codified design spans 10 sections covering plain-English copywriting rules R1–R10 (§1), a 20-row simplification mapping table (§2), the new tone as a personal AI command center with first-person headings and a no-`Date.now()` parser-purity guarantee (§3), an immersive visual style direction with layered glassmorphism / radial-ring hero / safety-lock dials / dot-strip timeline / two-column active-vs-resting layout / motion gated by `prefers-reduced-motion` / CSS-only animation with no WebGL / no JavaScript runtime / no external CDN font fetch / new palette additions (§4), a 7-step section redesign priority order (§5), the safety-content invariants preserving the verbatim trading-isolation disclaimer + SCAFFOLD-carried-forward Astro `define:vars` XSS advisory framing + parser-driven safety-lock data + neutral phrasing rule for symbol-like literals ("the armed-trading flag" / "Kraken credential env vars" / "the Railway deploy token" / "the GitHub token" / "the OpenAI key" / "subprocess-spawn APIs") + approvers exactly `{Victor}` + locked parser-purity invariant (§6), accessibility requirements covering WCAG AA contrast / skip-link / section aria-labelledby plumbing / motion gating / focus-visible / screen-reader equivalents on visuals / decorative SVG aria-hidden / system-stack font fallback / tap targets ≥ 44px / no autoplay (§7), file-edit targets restricted to `web/` (+ `web/tailwind.config.mjs`) with no new dependencies and no runtime fonts fetched (§8), untouchable file enumeration plus the four governance clauses RE-6 sealed-invariants block / RE-7 preview-host override / RE-8 codification governance / RE-9 parent-Relay evidence boundary (§9), and a full 24-item Codex review checklist for the future implementation phase (§10). The pushed WEB-UX-UPGRADE scope was exactly 27 web files: 20 tracked `web/` modifications (Card, Section, BaseLayout, 12 section components, 3 stylesheets, tailwind config, pages/index) + 7 new `web/src/components/` files (RadialRing, SafetyDial, StatusChip, CollapsibleList, MissionStatus, DecisionsNeeded, SystemBoundaries). Codex SAFE IMPLEMENTATION final review on the combined patch returned 19 of 20 source-verifiable goals PASS; the sole FAIL was Goal 15 build-attestation accepted by Victor as a documented procedural non-finding (review-contract constraint forbidding Codex from running `npm run build`, not a source defect). The build was operator-approved and ran at 17:39:55 local time: PASS in 697ms, 1 page built, 82,742-byte `web/dist/index.html`, CSS bundle `-GloXaHg.css`. Operator visually approved the resulting dashboard via the local preview server. The implementation delivered: friendlier mission-language translation tables across 11 components (raw IDs demoted to `.tech-id` captions + `title=""` hover tooltips); a "Current mission" focal banner with mission-pulse + scan-sweep above the hero; a promoted "Next objective" card with directional arrow + glow-swell; Mission Status command strip (6 cells derived from existing `safetyGates`); Decisions on Hold card (filtered backlog `BLOCKED-DECISION` items); System Boundaries 5-row panel (Governance / Runtime / Communication / Trading boundary / Final authority); grouped/collapsible Finished Work via native `<details>/<summary>` (9 sub-cascade groups); Past/Now/Future 3-column roadmap with cyan-scan-sweep on the Now column; 10 semantic state chips (PASS/DONE/OFF/DORMANT/BLOCKED/PAUSED/PLANNED/IN-PROGRESS/SHUT/ON); two glass-card tiers (primary closer, secondary deeper); deepened glassmorphism (blur 20px, radius 1.25rem, inner highlight); ambient background layer (3 drifting orbs + scan-overlay); demoted SHA chips throughout (55% opacity, 92% scale); "Why blocked" first-person rewrites; and ~1953 total inserted lines / 145 deleted across 27 files. Amendment landed verified smoke-test contract fixes, one defensive improvement, and one honest deferral: (a) synthetic `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN: 'smoke-test-synthetic-token-DO-NOT-USE'` added to every booting smoke test so `validateEnv()` does not halt class 21 before intended test paths execute; (b) new `buildPendingFilename()` helper in `synthetic-message.js` emits canonical timestamped filenames matching `src/store/source-of-truth.js` `PENDING_FILENAME_PATTERN`; `message_id` tightened to fit the `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,32}` body constraint; all fixture-write sites use the helper; (c) Case 8 sub-case assertion redirected from publish-log to `process.stdout.write` capture for the `safeLog`-emitted `secondaryHaltClass:24` + `moved:false` fields per `src/runtime/halt.js:202-211` emission surface; (d) Case 10 defensive `ExitSentinel` / `process.exit` stub added; asserts no unexpected halt fires on dry-run success path; (e) Case 12 deferred because the prior Path A read-only audit verified two source-grounded findings: `src/runtime/rate-limit-state.js:97-110` validates `channelRateLimits` entries as `{ maxPerWindow, windowMs }` objects and rejects `maxPerWindow <= 0`; `src/verify/limits.js:79-92` compares `current >= channelCap` where channelCap is the full object from `channelRateLimits[channel_name]`, not `channelCap.maxPerWindow`. This combined source-contract drift means the boot-pipeline rate-limit halt cannot currently prove RATE_LIMIT_HIT (halt class 5) using the documented object shape; Case 12 honestly deferred with a TODO documenting future remediation options. Codex review history: Codex DESIGN-ONLY round-1 (against the upstream `…-F-HALT-SMOKE-RUN-DESIGN`) returned FAIL with 5 source-grounded findings; operator-approved Path A READ-ONLY AUDIT verified all 5 findings TRUE against Relay source; `…-F-HALT-SMOKE-AMENDMENT` opened (Mode 4 SAFE IMPLEMENTATION) to fix the 5 verified drifts; Codex SAFE IMPLEMENTATION round-1 returned PASS WITH REQUIRED EDITS on Case 12 Option 12-D (`maxPerWindow: 0` does not work); operator-approved Path A CASE12-AUDIT verified both Codex Case 12 claims TRUE; operator-approved Option D applied (Case 12 replaced with `test.skip()` + TODO); Codex narrow round-2 not re-dispatched per operator decision (Codex round-1 conditionally authorized this path once Case 12 was resolved); amendment committed at Relay `590c1c9b42d96298c625df17ad892e7bf318c8ab` and pushed as fast-forward `abc7a71..590c1c9`. This is a fifth interface-contract drift NOT addressed by F-HALT-AMENDMENT (which fixed Bugs 1-4 in `boot.js`).
